programmes.show_description: Donald and Jacqueline take a trip to scatter Big Donna's ashes, whilst Joyce Temple-Savage takes a few days off to enjoy some male company. She leaves Lesley in charge, and chaos ensues. S6 E5
Arthur gets involved with a singer - when he and Terry have a disagreement, he also gets involved with the notorious Vic Piner. Can Terry rescue Arthur? S1 E9: Monday Night Fever
Alf is charged to look after the Christmas club subscriptions at the pub. Unfortunately maths is not his strong point and he struggles to balance the books. S6 E6: Christmas Club Books
Robin and Chrissy return home to find their front door open and the rent money that was on the sideboard missing. S1 E5: It's Only Money
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe
When Christmas finally arrives, presents are exchanged and the Osbournes are paid a visit by techno DJ Louis Osbourne, Ozzy's son from his first marriage. S1 E9: A Very Ozzy Christmas
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S7, E5: The One That Got Away
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S7, E6: The Visit
It's August Bank Holiday on the railway; with the sun predicted, it's set to be a busy and lucrative few days. S3 E4

Terry's job as security for the local Rotary Club offers Arthur a chance to gain some much-needed respectability when he is invited to speak at a function. S4 E8: Willesden Suite
Sarah announces to James that she is pregnant with his baby, but this on top of his gambling debts is the final straw for the family - can they avoid a major scandal? S2 E2: A Pair of Exiles
Jack and Adrian start their new jobs at the Boating Club, Aveline at the vet's and Ma at the dentist's, while Joey heads for the dole office. S4 E5
Harold meets a French au pair on his rounds and starts dating her, but a revelation from Albert's past means Harold's relationship with Monique may be too close for comfort. S3 E4: Steptoe a La Cart
British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
Alan's getting serious about Caroline, but her parents are well-to-do and Rigsby is far from convinced that the young scruff could possibly make the relationship work. S3 E6: The Cocktail Hour
While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love
Having reconciled himself to living in different eras with different sweethearts, Gary finds his lifestyle threatened when builders start to demolish his route to the past. S2 E10: Don't Fence Me In
Lady Caroline invites Jack to visit Gallowshield, Sarah is about to marry Stan Liddell, who is involved in anti-fascist politics, and Jack helps a persecuted Jewish shopkeeper. S4 E8: Action!
Terry's job as security for the local Rotary Club offers Arthur a chance to gain some much-needed respectability when he is invited to speak at a function. S4 E8: Willesden Suite
Sitcom about an on-again-off-again relationship. Vince upsets the female members of the Warrender family by pranging Penny's car. S3 E5: The Witness
When Chrissy is harassed by an intimidating tall man at the pub, Robin decides that he will learn self-defence and take the man on the next time he sees him. S2 E2: Colour Me Yellow
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. Frank spends the weekend wrecking his brother-in-law's hi-tech house. S1 E2: George's House
When Hacker recognises a visiting head of an African nation as someone he knew, he does his best to offload some British-made oil rigging gear on him. S1 E2: The Official Visit
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold meets a French au pair on his rounds and starts dating her, but a revelation from Albert's past means Harold's relationship with Monique may be too close for comfort. S3 E4: Steptoe a La Cart
When Miss Jones raises suspicions about new tenant Madeline, Rigsby is not too bothered about her brand of 'modern interpretive dancing' but isn't that keen on her partner. S3 E5: Fawcett's Python
Terry considers leaving to get a job in Berwick when appalled by the changes in Newcastle, but his friend's wanderlust has set Bob thinking. S1 E4: Moving On
Arthur ventures into the wine trade with a vintage from the Brothers of the Blessed Bidolph - a small monastic order just off the A2. S8 E2: A Bouquet of Barbed Wine

Madge has a new outlook on life, after receiving some worrying news from Dr Kundu. Encouraged by Madge and Liam, Kenneth decides to go on a health kick. Cyril organises a Man v Food competition. S6 E6
When Paddy's caught on camera for an armed robbery, Mimi begs him to call his brother, Noel. But Paddy has a dark history with his sibling and would rather go to jail. S5 E11
Robin is conned by his friend Larry into looking after a puppy but has to hide it from George - who operates a No Animals rule - until he can find a new home for it. S1 E7: No Children, No Dogs
When Miss Jones becomes engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out, Rigsby is distraught but is determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses

Madge has a new outlook on life, after receiving some worrying news from Dr Kundu. Encouraged by Madge and Liam, Kenneth decides to go on a health kick. Cyril organises a Man v Food competition. S6 E6
Terry is working in a restaurant and trying to protect Charlie and Christina from a family who will stop at nothing to close it. S1 E10: The Dessert Song
Else sends a letter inviting everyone to Australia - everyone but Alf, that is. S6 E7: The Letter
When George has a nasty cold and gives it to Robin, Chrissy and Jo try to make him better using a range of home-made remedies. S1 E6: Match of the Day
David is less than happy to find that the obnoxious Kev Wilson, a man who has always done better than him and let him know it since childhood, is staying at the hotel. S2 E6: Snap
Ozzy and Sharon are invited to Washington, D.C. for a dinner at the White House, and Kelly performs her cover version of Madonna's 'Papa, Don't Preach' at the MTV Movie Awards. S2 E1: What Goes Up
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S7, E7: Far From The Madding Pong
Nellie takes Eli along to see The Doctor, only The Doctor is more interested in Nellie's behaviour, than Eli's cough. S3 E1: What Seems To Be The Trouble
One of the biggest events of the year - War Weekend - takes place, but the event's centrepiece, a WWII artillery gun, is putting up a fight. S3 E5

Arthur plans to become a major player in the fitness industry while Terry gets involved with two Italian families when one of their daughters walks out on her husband. S4 E9: Windows
The Kirbridge marriage is in trouble as Elizabeth is feeling unwanted by Lawrence and he refuses to discuss her want to start a family - does the poet have other interests? S2 E3: Married Love
Aveline brings home a sick kestrel, while Roxy arrives on the doorstep with her cat and her belongings. What will turn up next? S4 E6
The Steptoes go for their weekly visit to the cinema; unfortunately, their tastes in big screen entertainment differ wildly. S3 E5: Sunday For Seven Days
There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
The house is plunged into confusion and mourning when Rigsby discovers basement tenant Mr Osborne stiff as a board. S3 E7: Suddenly At Home

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow

In the run-up to New Year's Eve, Gary worries about finding a way to see in the New Year with both Phoebe and Yvonne. Mrs Bloss threatens to chuck him out of his bedsit. S3 E2: It Ain't Necessarily So

Billy treats a son of Jack's old sergeant Fred at his London practice; Jessie and Billy get him to persuade Jack to smuggle guns past Franco's blockade to help the Republicans. S4 E9: Comrades in Arms
Arthur plans to become a major player in the fitness industry while Terry gets involved with two Italian families when one of their daughters walks out on her husband. S4 E9: Windows
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Things start to look bright for the Warrenders as Norman's job prospects improve. S3 E6: Employment Prospects
Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise of Older Men
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. Crossing the road and phoning the doctor seems straightforward enough - but not for Frank. S1 E3: Love Thy Neighbour
Fired up by the press, Hacker decides to impose cuts on the Civil Service - however, Sir Humphrey is appalled and sets out to stop him. S1 E3: The Economy Drive

Terry tries to track down some of his old flames, but probing into the past produces embarrassing results. S1 E5: I'll Never Forget Whatshername
When his wife goes missing, Arthur is under suspicion, but he's got a more important cash flow matter to contend with. S8 E3: Whatever Happened to Her Indoors?
Solana CEO, Crystal Hennessy-Vass (Joan Collins), arrives at the Solana for an inspection, and to give Joyce a dressing down. Donald and Jacqueline are visited by an old friend (Rusty Lee). S6 E7
When Stan gets caught in the crossfire between Yvonne and her kids, a full-blown row puts their relationship on the rocks S5 E12
The flatmates plan a party to take advantage of the fact that the Ropers will be out visiting Mildred's sister and her husband; the evening begins well but turns sour. S2 E1: While the Cat's Away
Rigsby has found a new lodger called Seymour who is staying in town away from his country estate, but Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman
